Author: XeldarAlz
Name: Doman Mahjong Solver
Punchline: Doman Mahjong helper — hints or auto-play.
Description:
This plugin is in alpha — expect bugs. Please report any you find, and if you'd like to contribute, join in!

A helper for Doman Mahjong at the Gold Saucer. Two modes:

• Hint mode (default): shows the best tile to discard and a short explanation. You still click every move.
• Auto-play: the plugin watches the table and clicks for you with natural pacing.

Starts off by default. You must accept a short notice before auto-play can be turned on, and you can switch back to hints or turn it off at any time from the plugin window.
Changelog:
v0.1.0.8 — Hotfix on top of v0.1.0.7. Users were stuck at state-6 hand=14 with auto-play discarding the same tile every 3 seconds and the game state never advancing — the v0.1.0.7 observability captured the exact repeating pattern in the findings stream and the corpus pinned the root cause inside the hour. The fix: at state-6 SelfDeclareList with no call modal visible, the addon shows the closed hand as the primary clickable surface and expects FireCallback([15, tile_texture_id]) rather than the classic FireCallback([7, slot_index]) — opcode 7 silently no-ops on that UI shape, which is why the dispatcher kept reporting Ok while the game ignored every click. Corpus evidence: 226 game-source records of opcode-15 with [tileTextureBase + tile_id] in the inputs telemetry, matching every manual user discard. The dispatcher now reads the slot's raw texture id (akadora bit preserved) and routes via opcode 15 when state=6 + no list-widget modal, falling back to opcode 7 for state-30 normal discards. Closes the auto-play regression reproducing on 2026-05-19.

v0.1.0.7 — Major auto-play correctness fix. Call-prompt accept dispatch was hardcoded to button index 0, which silently force-fired Pon on every Pon+Chi simultaneous prompt and picked the leftmost chi variant on every multi-chi prompt regardless of what the policy chose. The dispatcher now computes the correct button index per ActionKind (Pon → Chi variants → MinKan → ShouMinKan → Ron → Riichi → Tsumo → Pass) and the variant reader emits Pon+Chi+Kan candidates simultaneously instead of dropping the non-leftmost ones — multi-chi variant selection now actually respects the policy's choice. State-28 list-widget call prompts (novice tables) now derive candidates from the hand instead of silently passing. Akadora (red 5m/5p/5s) now counts toward dora han whether the red is in the closed hand or moved into an open meld; previously dropped at the meld boundary. The Debug overlay's Status tab gains a live Call-prompt analysis card showing flags / candidate counts / computed accept-button index for every offered action — verify decoding correctness at a glance during in-game testing. Findings telemetry now ships decision / dispatch_attempted / hand_state_paused records so the next regression triages from one corpus grep. Memory dumps include an AgentEmj snapshot — the per-seat discard tile arrays live there, not in the addon range, unblocking opponent-view reverse-engineering. The first-attach lifecycle finding now fires from the Poll discovery path too, fixing ~70% of installs that previously shipped zero gameplay logs because the plugin loaded with the addon already open. Tsumo opcode 9 graduated from speculative to corpus-confirmed (14 distinct installs).

v0.1.0.6 — Further observability while the auto-play stall is being root-caused. Plugin-load line now includes the assembly version so dalamud.log proves which build is running. Both the discard-dispatch and call-dispatch bodies now log entry (state/hand/flags/choice/tile) and exit (LastActionDescription with dispatch result). Previously the policy-discard path set LastActionDescription only for the UI and never logged to Plugin.Log, leaving the dispatch invisible in dalamud.log. No behavior change — purely observability.

v0.1.0.5 — Hotfix for the auto-play stall on every discard turn. State-6 hand=14 is the SelfDeclareList popup that appears after every draw, offering Riichi/Tsumo/AnKan AND listing your closed hand as discardable items. AutoPlay was treating it as a pure call-prompt and pressing Pass when the policy returned Discard, which dismissed the offer without discarding — game stayed at state-6 hand=14 forever, every 3 seconds another Pass. The dispatch path now routes Discard/Riichi choices through the list-widget click handler (SelectItem) instead of falling through to Pass. Found via v0.1.0.4 diagnostic logging in under 5 minutes of live repro.

v0.1.0.4 — Observability hotfix. The auto-play loop now logs one Plugin.Log.Info line per skip-reason transition so dalamud.log answers "why didn't auto-play fire" without manual repro. Gate states (TosAccepted / AutomationArmed / SuggestionOnly), stuck-dispatch detection, snapshot unavailability, non-actionable states, and retry-debounce suppression all surface as named transitions. Dedups by exact reason string so a steady gate-bail logs once, not 60×/sec.

v0.1.0.3 — Hotfix on top of 0.1.0.2. The new closed-hand-delta meld inference compared opp discard counts to the previous tick, but opponents discard several ticks BEFORE we click chi/pon (their discard → prompt visible for seconds → our click). By the time the closed hand shrank, the comparison saw no increment and inference never fired, leaving 0.1.0.2 users in the same chi/pon stall the rewrite was meant to fix. The tracker now latches the most-recent opp discarder when it happens and holds it across the prompt window until the closed-hand shrink consumes it. Two regression tests pin the realistic timing.

v0.1.0.2 — Hotfix for the auto-play stall after chi/pon. Melds are now inferred from closed-hand deltas tick-by-tick (chi/pon = hand shrinks by 2 with an opponent discard increment; minkan = shrinks by 3). The previous FireCallback-payload heuristic missed every prompt shape that put the call at option 1+ (multi-variant chi, pon+chi simultaneous) and every state-28 list-widget chi, leaving the scorer staring at an 11-tile hand with melds=0 and pausing hints / auto-play for the rest of the hand. The new path is variant-agnostic and click-payload-agnostic — works on both Emj and EmjL. Closes #34, #37.

v0.1.0.1 — Hotfix. Auto-play no longer freezes after a chi (post-call discard popup is now routed through the list-widget click path; the previous opcode-7 FireCallback returned false on that shell and the loop got stuck for the rest of the hand). Findings telemetry no longer ships absolute filesystem paths — anything before XIVLauncher / xlcore / Roaming is stripped before upload. Memory-dump capture skips ~30% of idle-cadence frames (AtkValuesCount < 100), trimming bandwidth without losing any gameplay-relevant snapshots.

v0.1.0.0 — Architectural overhaul. Renamed plugin internal name (DomanMahjongAI → Mahjong.Plugin.Dalamud) — first launch will start with a fresh config; reaccept the ToS notice and re-arm whichever mode you want. Engine, policy, and rules layers split out as standalone projects. Native discard-capture hook gains an addon-poll fallback for clients where the sigscan misses. Heavily tested: 521 unit tests across the libraries and plugin shell.
